select_when: Uncertainty is quantifiable enough that ranges, distributions, or sampling logic can improve the decision.
The team needs to distinguish signal from noise rather than argue from anecdotes, vivid cases, or gut feel.
A problem benefits from calibrated estimation, comparison, or inference instead of loose narrative certainty.
Background rates, sampling structure, or distribution shape matter more than one compelling example.
avoid_when: Statistical language is being used decoratively and no one has checked whether the data quality, assumptions, or sample structure justify the conclusion.
The team is leaning on the mean outcome while ignoring extreme outcomes, missingness, or failure distributions that matter more to the decision.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on distributions, inference, and calibration rather than broad math rhetoric or generic data enthusiasm.
donor_drops: Did not widen into advanced analytics tooling because the raw source is stronger on base rates, signal detection, and useful statistical judgment.
open_questions: The typed output compresses several surfaces, including estimation, sampling checks, and inference quality.

select_when.note: The source explicitly supports quantifiable uncertainty, signal-versus-noise work, and calibrated estimation rather than anecdotal reasoning.

avoid_when.note: Both avoid conditions come directly from the dangers section on decorative statistics and over-reliance on the mean.

input_type.note: The source activates on noise-versus-signal judgment problems, while the compact label is reviewed normalization.

output_type.note: The playbook yields calibrated statistical interpretation; the output label compresses that surface into a reusable artifact.

reasoning_types.note: The model is explicitly probabilistic and diagnostic in how it distinguishes data signal, assumptions, and inferential strength.
